title: PUBLIC SAFETY POWER SHUTOFFS - 211info
text: COOS-CURRY ELECTRIC COOPERATIVE PUBLIC SAFETY POWER SHUTOFFS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A Public Safety Power Shutoff (PSPS) is an operational practice an electric utility may use to shut off power in high-risk areas to help prevent wildfires during extreme and dangerous weather conditions. PSPS means Coos-Curry Electric Cooperative will shut off power, and it is considered an action of last resort to help protect public safety. 43050 Highway 101 Port Orford, OR 97465 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 332-3931 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 332-3931 24/7 Outage & Information Line (866) 352-9044 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None AREA SERVED: Coos, Curry, and Douglas Counties Services: Electric Service Connection/Repair * Disaster/Emergency Issues If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. COOS-CURRY ELECTRIC COOPERATIVE
